title: Integration tests self-skip when data/kaggle is absent
evidence: internal/soccer/integration_test.go:18 t.Skipf("data directory not available") — guard in dataDir(); data IS bundled here so TestLoadRealData/TestRealDataQueries run, but the skip path silently drops end-to-end coverage in any env without the CSVs
suggestion: Keep the guard but emit a non-skip failure in CI where data is expected, or use a tiny committed fixture so the integration path always executes

title: TDD methodology followed — thorough test-first coverage
evidence: 44 Test* functions across 9 *_test.go files (server_test.go, tools_test.go, query_test.go, loader_test.go, normalize_test.go, canonical_test.go, dedup_test.go, integration_test.go); every query-engine and parser function has a unit test; test_coverage=0.5663 with defect_rate=1.0 (build+tests pass)

title: Line coverage is 56.6% despite broad test breadth
evidence: scores.json test_coverage=0.5663333; format.go (answer formatting) and parts of server.go transport loop are exercised indirectly, leaving untested branches
suggestion: Add direct tests for format.go output strings and error-path branches to lift coverage; not a spec gap
